input.background: Heights of 10-year-old male children follow a Normal distribution with μ=138 centimeters and σ=7 centimeters.
input.question: Use the 68-95—99.7 rule to determine the middle 68% of values. What percent of values fall below this range? What percent fall above this range?

output.answer: According to the 68 part of the 68-95-99.7 rule, 68% of values will fall in this range. The remaining 32% fall outside of this range, with an equal number at either extreme because of symmetry; 16% of values will fall below 131 and 16% will be above 145.

mean_height.description: The mean height of 10-year-old male children in centimeters.

std_dev_height.description: The standard deviation of the height of 10-year-old male children in centimeters.

middle_68_percent_range.description: The range of heights that includes the middle 68% of values based on the 68-95-99.7 rule.

percent_below_range.description: The percentage of values that fall below the middle 68% range.

percent_above_range.description: The percentage of values that fall above the middle 68% range.
